text.title: Pricing Strategy Real Examples
text.summary: Two concrete examples — SaaS tool and info product — with value analysis, pricing decision, and outcome data.

example.note: Situation: project management tool for small teams. Value analysis led to target price of 5–10% of value delivered.
example.code:
Value analysis: Saves 5 hours/week per team = $1,000/month value Replaces 3 tools = $150/month savings Total value delivered: ~$1,150/month
Target price (5-10% of value): $57–115/month
Decision: Pro tier: $79/month (full features, primary revenue) Basic tier: $29/month (individuals, funnel entry)
Results: 60% choose Pro 30% choose Basic 10% generate enterprise inquiries

example.note: Situation: one-time purchase course for developers. Anchored to salary increase rather than comparable course prices.
example.code:
Value analysis: Skills learned lead to ~$20K annual salary increase Comparable courses: $500–2,000 Time to ROI: 6 months
Decision: Anchor: "$20K salary increase" in headline Price: $997 one-time Payment plan: 3 x $397
Results: 70% chose payment plan 4% conversion from landing page (standard for this price range)
